title: Jared Spataro's Post - LinkedIn
full_text:
I’ve talked before about #AI-native companies—businesses built around AI from the start—and this The Wall Street Journal article does a great job exploring what makes these organizations successful.
I’ve talked before about #AI-native companies—businesses built around AI from the start—and this The Wall Street Journal article does a great job exploring what makes these organizations successful. Unlike traditional firms adopting AI, these companies leverage it as their foundation, dynamically learning and adapting to customer needs like custom-built applications.
From marketing and advertising to software development and finance, AI-native startups are pushing boundaries and redefining what's possible. Their advantage? A compounding effect—AI-driven experiences improve over time, creating competitive moats that are hard to replicate. Established companies should take note: AI-native firms offer not only competition but also valuable lessons in agility and innovation.
Thanks to WSJ for the great write-up and for including my perspective.
AI-Native Companies Are Growing Fast and Doing Things Differently
